Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Vector Meson Dominance 15 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Lemonade by AMD: a fast and open source local LLM server using GPU and NPU 493 upvotes, 107 comments
  3. George Goble has died 139 upvotes, 26 comments
  4. C89cc.sh – standalone C89/ELF64 compiler in pure portable shell 104 upvotes, 20 comments
  5. Google releases Gemma 4 open models 1357 upvotes, 390 comments
  6. Maze Algorithms (1997) 38 upvotes, 5 comments
  7. Prefer do notation over Applicative operators when assembling records (2024) 42 upvotes, 6 comments
  8. Significant progress made on Xbox 360 recompilation 103 upvotes, 22 comments
  9. Pentagon Pete's Bigoted Reason for Firing Top General Leaks 15 upvotes, 1 comments
  10. Qwen3.6-Plus: Towards real world agents 494 upvotes, 175 comments
  11. Decisions that eroded trust in Azure – by a former Azure Core engineer 611 upvotes, 239 comments
  12. LinkedIn is searching your browser extensions 1664 upvotes, 696 comments
  13. JSON Canvas Spec (2024) 100 upvotes, 32 comments
  14. The True Shape of Io's Steeple Mountain 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  15. Good ideas do not need lots of lies in order to gain public acceptance (2008) 229 upvotes, 90 comments
  16. A Few Good Magazines From the 70s and 80s 52 upvotes, 15 comments
  17. Tor Alva: The Tallest 3D-Printed Building in the World 27 upvotes, 6 comments
  18. Artemis II's toilet is a moon mission milestone 213 upvotes, 88 comments
  19. Inside Nepal's Fake Rescue Racket 277 upvotes, 120 comments
  20. Sweden goes back to basics, swapping screens for books in the classroom 806 upvotes, 397 comments
  21. Tailscale's new macOS home 422 upvotes, 203 comments
  22. Show HN: Made a little Artemis II tracker 82 upvotes, 31 comments
  23. Memo: A language that remembers only the last 12 lines of code 44 upvotes, 19 comments
  24. Post Mortem: axios NPM supply chain compromise 84 upvotes, 44 comments
  25. The Joy of Numbered Streets 37 upvotes, 16 comments
  26. Show HN: Home Maker: Declare Your Dev Tools in a Makefile 30 upvotes, 14 comments
  27. Artemis computer running two instances of MS outlook; they can't figure out why 381 upvotes, 290 comments
  28. Cursor 3 374 upvotes, 303 comments
  29. OpenAI Acquires TBPN 198 upvotes, 158 comments
